petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented individual (referred to as 'she') seeking classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No specific information about her field of work, nationality, or career qualifications is provided in the decision. The Vermont Service Center director denied the petition, finding she had not established the sustained national or international acclaim necessary for the classification.
outcome_reasoning: The AAO summarily dismissed the appeal because the petitioner's appellate submission was unaccompanied by any arguments or evidence addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3). The petitioner indicated she was not submitting a separate brief or evidence, and did not specifically address the reasons stated for denial or provide any additional evidence. Under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), an appeal shall be summarily dismissed if the party concerned fails to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal.
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: the petitioner failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact on appeal, failed to submit a brief or additional evidence, and did not address the reasons for denial, resulting in summary dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v).
